name: Hearty Celery Soup (Dupree)
recipeInstructions:
1. Select thick-sliced country-style peppercured bacon.
2. In a large skillet, fry the bacon until crisp. Drain on paper towels, crumble into small pieces, and set aside. Pour off all but about 4 tablespoons of the bacon drippings.
3. Add the onions, celery, and garlic to the skillet and sauté until the onions begin to turn golden brown, about 10 minutes.
4. In a large stockpot, bring the chicken broth to a boil. Add the sautéd vegetables and cook for about 20 minutes, or until tender.
5. Strain the vegetable solids, reserving the broth, and puree them in a blender or food processor until smooth. Return the purée to the broth and add the milk, celery seeds, and thyme.
6. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to a simmer, and cook 5 minutes longer.
7. Adjust seasonings with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Serve sprinkled with reserved bacon, cheese, and celery leaves.
Yields 8 servings
